div1.entryFree: Unloved, not loved: Mids. III, 2, 234. the ostentation of our love, which, left unshown, is often left u. Ant. III, 6, 53 (i. e. not felt; to love a love being a similar phrase as f. i. to think a thought. cf. what ruins are in me . . . by him not ruined? Err. II, 1, 96. the want that you have wanted, Lr. I, 1, 282).
